skill.name: cicd-automation-workflow-automate
skill.description: You are a workflow automation expert specializing in creating efficient CI/CD pipelines, GitHub Actions workflows, and automated development processes. Design automation that reduces manual work, i...
skill.summary: CI/CD workflow automation expert for building efficient pipelines, GitHub Actions, and automated development processes
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate CI/CD workflow automation tool. All static findings are false positives. The detected patterns (external commands, network URLs, environment access) are standard and expected for a CI/CD skill. Credentials are handled via GitHub Actions secrets, which is the secure standard pattern.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution
medium_findings.description: Skill contains shell commands for running builds, tests, and deployments. These are standard CI/CD patterns (npm, docker, terraform, git). Arguments are hardcoded or reference GitHub Actions context, not user input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - these are legitimate CI/CD commands (npm ci, docker build, terraform apply) used in standard DevOps workflows

medium_findings.title: Network URL References
medium_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs point to public services like npm registries, GitHub Actions, Helm repositories. These are legitimate infrastructure endpoints, not exfiltration targets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - URLs are public services like prometheus-community Helm repo, not malicious endpoints

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
medium_findings.description: Accesses process.env and configuration files. All credential references use GitHub Actions secrets pattern (secrets.*) which is the secure standard.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - credentials use secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN, secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID pattern which is encrypted and secure in GitHub Actions

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Operations
medium_findings.description: File I/O operations for reading config files, writing documentation, creating Docker networks. Standard CI/CD operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - operations are standard file I/O for CI/CD: reading configs, writing docs, caching dependencies, copying .env.example templates

low_findings.title: Configuration File Parsing
low_findings.description: Python code parses YAML configuration files for workflow detection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: False positive - standard yaml.safe_load() for reading workflow configs, not sensitive configuration

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers and DevOps engineers create efficient CI/CD pipelines and GitHub Actions workflows. It reduces manual deployment work and improves consistency across development, staging, and production environments.
actual_capabilities: Design and create GitHub Actions workflows with multiple stages
Implement CI/CD pipelines with build, test, and deploy steps
Set up automated release workflows with semantic versioning
Add security scanning and compliance checks to pipelines
limitations: Does not execute actual deployments - provides guidance and code templates only
Requires existing GitHub repository and appropriate permissions
Cannot access external CI/CD systems beyond GitHub Actions
Templates require customization for specific project needs

use_cases.title: New Project CI Setup
use_cases.description: Set up automated testing and code quality checks for a new Node.js project using GitHub Actions
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers starting a new project

use_cases.title: Production Deployment Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a multi-environment deployment pipeline with staging and production approvals

use_cases.title: Infrastructure as Code
use_cases.description: Implement Terraform workflows for managing cloud infrastructure through GitOps

prompt_templates.title: Basic CI Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for a Node.js project that runs tests on every pull request and deploys to staging on merge to main.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up continuous integration for a web application

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Environment Deploy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a deployment pipeline with development, staging, and production environments. Include approval gates for production and automatic rollbacks on failure.

prompt_templates.title: Release Automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up automated semantic versioning and GitHub releases that trigger when code is merged to main branch.

prompt_templates.title: Security Scanning
prompt_templates.prompt: Add security scanning steps to our CI pipeline including dependency vulnerability checks, secret scanning, and code analysis.

output_examples.input: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs tests and lints code on every push
output_examples.output: A complete YAML workflow file with steps for checking out code, setting up Node.js, installing dependencies, running lint and test commands, and uploading test results

output_examples.input: Design a deployment workflow with staging and production environments
output_examples.output: Multi-stage pipeline diagram showing development → staging → production flow with approval gates and environment-specific configurations

output_examples.input: Set up automated releases with semantic versioning
output_examples.output: Release configuration and workflow that automatically bumps version numbers, generates changelogs, and creates GitHub releases based on commit messages

best_practices: Use GitHub Actions caching to speed up workflows and reduce costs
Separate concerns with multiple jobs for testing, building, and deploying
Always include rollback procedures for production deployments
Store sensitive credentials in GitHub encrypted secrets, never in workflow files
anti_patterns: Avoid hardcoding secrets or API keys directly in workflow files
Do not skip approval gates for production deployments
Avoid running full deployments on every commit - use separate stages
Do not ignore test failures - workflows should fail fast on errors

faq.question: Does this skill execute deployments?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and code templates for creating workflows. It does not directly execute deployments but helps you design safe automation.

faq.question: What CI/CD platforms does this support?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on GitHub Actions but also covers patterns applicable to GitLab CI, Jenkins, and other CI/CD systems.

faq.question: How do I handle secrets and credentials?
faq.answer: Use GitHub encrypted secrets (secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN) in your workflow files. Never hardcode credentials.

faq.question: Can this help with infrastructure automation?
faq.answer: Yes, it includes Terraform workflow patterns for infrastructure as code and Kubernetes deployment automation.

faq.question: Is this suitable for production deployments?
faq.answer: Yes, with proper approval gates, rollback plans, and security scanning as recommended in the skill.

faq.question: What programming languages does this support?
faq.answer: The workflows are language-agnostic but include examples for Node.js, Python, and Go projects.
